trigger: Bryan asked for a massive script automation audit and authorized changes without additional approvals.
findings: scripts/check-automation-contract.ps1 needed strict live QoL validation plus a repo snapshot fallback for CI and repo-only contexts.
The repo had overlapping CI workflows: the primary CI workflow ran only tests, while alpha-ci ran the broader npm check rail.
npm is not available on this local shell PATH, so direct bundled Node validation was required locally.

changes.summary: Added repo snapshot fallback, strict -RequireLive mode, snapshot prompt hash validation, clearer failure output, and pass counts for live versus snapshot validation.
Resolved relative output paths against the repository root so snapshot generation is stable from different working directories.
Added a Node wrapper that locates PowerShell and runs project PowerShell scripts with non-interactive flags.
Added automation check scripts and included automation contract validation in npm run check.
Consolidated CI into one full Windows-based check workflow.
Removed duplicate alpha-only workflow after primary CI became the full check rail.
Updated automation runbook, alpha framework, milestones, backlog, memory, decision log, audit log, and run log to reflect the new check behavior.

validation: Bundled Node scripts/run-powershell-script.mjs scripts\check-automation-contract.ps1 passed for 10 QoL automations from live TOMLs.
known_limits: Full npm run check was not runnable in this local shell because npm is not on PATH.
Snapshot fallback is repo validation, not proof that the local Codex GUI registry currently has the ten expected live QoL automations.

self_improvement.evidence: The audit found reproducible automation checker failure in repo-only/local-missing-live contexts and duplicate CI check surfaces.

next_actions: Use npm run check:automation:live or scripts/check-automation-contract.ps1 -RequireLive when the local Codex automation registry itself is under audit.
Keep default automation contract checks snapshot-fallback aware so CI and repo-only audits can still validate prompt snapshots and supporting contracts.
